articles.title: Over 800 South Sudanese among 1,330 students sitting Sudan certificate exams in Juba

articles.context: The ongoing conflict in South Sudan, which erupted in 2013, has led to significant displacement and educational disruptions for many South Sudanese. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to restore peace and stability, yet challenges persist. The participation of South Sudanese students in Sudan's examination system highlights the complex interdependence between the two nations, particularly in education, amidst ongoing political and economic instability.

articles.title: ارتفاع سعر تحويل الجنيه السوداني مقابل الجنية المصري الثلاثاء 14 ابريل 2026

articles.context: تشهد السودان أزمة اقتصادية خانقة منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في 15 أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تدهور قيمة الجنيه السوداني. في ظل هذه الظروف، يعتمد الكثيرون على التحويلات المالية من مصر، حيث تزايدت الحاجة إلى الخدمات الإلكترونية بسبب شح السيولة. هذه الديناميكية تعكس التوترات المستمرة وتأثيرها على الحياة اليومية للسودانيين.

articles.title: السودان يحقق طفرة رقمية عبر منصة بلدنا
articles.summary: في تحول يصفه مراقبون بأنه بداية لعصر رقمي جديد في السودان، أعلن وزير التحول الرقمي والاتصالات أحمد الدرديري أن منصة “بلدنا” حققت نتائج لافتة خلال الأشهر الثلاثة الماضية، حيث تجاوز عدد المعاملات الناجحة حاجز المئة ألف معاملة. الوزير أوضح في تغريدة على منصة “إكس” أن زمن إنجاز المعاملات انخفض بشكل كبير من عشرين يوماً إلى …

articles.summary: نشرت الإعلامية والكاتبة الصحفية داليا الياس، كشفاً كاملاً بالتنقلات والترقيات داخل القوات المسلحة السودانية. وجاء الكشف متضمناً قرارات أصدرها القائد العام الفريق أول عبد الفتاح البرهان، شملت تغييرات بارزة في مواقع قيادية، إلى جانب إحالات للتقاعد وتعيينات في قطاعات مختلفة. الكشف الذي نشرته داليا الياس تضمن أسماء ضباط تمت ترقيتهم أو نقلهم إلى مواقع جديدة …
articles.context: تأتي هذه التنقلات الجديدة داخل القوات المسلحة السودانية في وقت حساس، حيث تتصاعد التوترات بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023. هذه التغييرات، التي أقرها الفريق أول عبد الفتاح البرهان، قد تؤثر على توازن القوى داخل الجيش وتعيد تشكيل الديناميات السياسية في البلاد، في ظل جهود المجتمع الدولي لإيجاد حل للأزمة السياسية المستمرة منذ الإطاحة بعمر البشير في 2019.

articles.title: رئيس حزب الأمة القومي يصدر قراراً بتعيين نائباً أول للرئيس

articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في وقت حساس للسودان، حيث يواجه الحزب تحديات سياسية كبيرة بعد أحداث 25 أكتوبر 2021، عندما تم تنفيذ انقلاب عسكري أدى إلى تفكيك الحكومة الانتقالية. حزب الأمة القومي، الذي يعد من أبرز الأحزاب السياسية في السودان، يسعى لتعزيز وحدته الداخلية وتفعيل دوره في العملية السياسية، خاصة مع استمرار الاحتجاجات الشعبية والمطالبات بإعادة الحكم المدني.

articles.title: موظفو مطارات السودان يرفضون قرار دعم السكن ويطالبون بالعدالة

articles.context: تأتي هذه الاحتجاجات في سياق الأوضاع الاقتصادية الصعبة التي يواجهها السودان بعد الثورة الشعبية في 2019، والتي أدت إلى الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير. تزايدت التوترات بين الحكومة الانتقالية والعاملين في القطاع العام، خاصة مع استمرار الصراعات في مناطق مثل دارفور والنيل الأزرق. كما أن قرار وزارة المالية يعكس التحديات المتعلقة بتوزيع الموارد في ظل الأزمات المتعددة التي يعاني منها البلد.

articles.title: بنك السودان المركزي يحدد موعداً نهائياً لتداول عملة الـ1000 والـ500 القديمة

articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في سياق جهود بنك السودان المركزي لتعزيز الاستقرار الاقتصادي في البلاد بعد سنوات من النزاع السياسي والاقتصادي، خاصة بعد ثورة ديسمبر 2018 التي أدت إلى تغييرات كبيرة في الحكومة. كما أن استبدال العملة القديمة يعكس محاولات الحكومة الانتقالية لمكافحة التضخم وتحسين الثقة في النظام المالي، وسط تحديات اقتصادية مستمرة منذ انفصال جنوب السودان في 2011 وتداعيات جائحة كوفيد-19.

articles.title: US Embassy raises concerns over transparency in mining sector

The Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, signed in September 2018, aimed to establish peace and promote economic development. However, persistent corruption and lack of regulatory frameworks in the mining sector hinder foreign investment, prompting the U.S. Embassy's recent concerns about transparency and governance.

articles.context: منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في 15 أبريل 2023، شهد السودان تدهوراً حاداً في الأوضاع الإنسانية والصحية. أدى هذا الصراع إلى انهيار النظام الصحي وظهور سوق للأدوية المهربة والمغشوشة، مما زاد من معاناة المرضى. في ظل غياب الرقابة الحكومية، استغل تجار الحرب الفوضى لتحقيق مكاسب مالية على حساب حياة المواطنين.

articles.title: Treasury targets law change to tame white elephant projects
articles.summary: Over 440 development projects valued at Sh662.9 billion are stalled across the country.
articles.context: Kenya's stalled development projects, valued at Sh662.9 billion, reflect ongoing challenges in governance and corruption that have plagued the country since the 2010 Constitution aimed to decentralize power. The recent push by the Treasury to amend laws governing public projects comes in the wake of President William Ruto's administration, which has prioritized economic recovery and accountability since taking office in September 2022, amid rising public discontent over mismanaged funds and unfulfilled promises.

articles.title: I don’t know… It wasn't me

articles.context: The article highlights the ongoing political tensions in Kenya, particularly following the 2022 general elections that saw President William Ruto come to power amid allegations of electoral malpractice. Wandayi, a prominent opposition figure and head of the Ministry of Information, has faced scrutiny over governance issues, reflecting the broader struggle between the ruling party and opposition factions. This dynamic is intensified by ongoing debates over accountability and transparency in government operations.

articles.title: Tribunal backs KPA in Sh1.3bn tenders fight with Finnish firm

articles.context: The dispute between the Kenya Ports Authority (KPA) and Finnish firm Kalmar stems from a broader context of procurement challenges in Kenya, particularly highlighted by the 2019 Public Procurement and Asset Disposal Act. This legislation aimed to enhance transparency but has faced scrutiny over its implementation. The tribunal's decision comes amid ongoing efforts to modernize Kenya's port operations, crucial for trade in the region, especially following the 2021 launch of the Nairobi-Mombasa Expressway.

articles.title: From Jomo Kenyatta to William Ruto, her voice has carried through five decades

As Kenya grapples with economic challenges and political polarization, Chesang's voice serves as a reminder of the cultural resilience amidst ongoing societal shifts.

articles.title: Ruto men on ODM’s radar as tensions rise
articles.summary: ODM MPs are allegedly being promised campaign money on condition they join UDA.

This maneuvering comes amidst a backdrop of shifting alliances and the upcoming 2027 elections, heightening the stakes for both parties in the political landscape.

articles.title: How Covid killed Nakuru’s Kanu street nightlife
articles.summary: Covid curfews emptied the roads as Nakuru’s busiest nightlife strip became a ghost of itself.
articles.context: Nakuru, a key city in Kenya's Rift Valley, has historically been a vibrant hub for nightlife, attracting both locals and tourists. However, the enforcement of Covid-19 curfews in March 2020 drastically impacted businesses, particularly along Kanu Street, which was known for its bustling bars and entertainment venues. As the country gradually reopened, ongoing economic challenges and political tensions ahead of the 2022 general elections further complicated the recovery of this vital social scene.

articles.title: Confessions of granny addicted to Aviator gambling platform

articles.context: In Kenya, the rise of online gambling platforms like Aviator has coincided with a surge in addiction cases, particularly among vulnerable populations. In 2021, the Betting Control and Licensing Board tightened regulations, but enforcement remains weak, exacerbating the plight of individuals like the grandmother in this story, who is battling both addiction and cancer.

articles.title: Weapon of mass murder: AK-47 linked to terror deaths
articles.summary: The AK-47 rifle accounted for many of the 847 deaths stemming from terror attacks in Kenya.
articles.context: The AK-47 has become a symbol of violence in Kenya, particularly in the context of the ongoing threat from Al-Shabaab, which has intensified since the group’s 2013 Westgate Mall attack. The Kenyan government has struggled to combat this insurgency, leading to a surge in terror-related deaths, with 847 fatalities reported in recent years. The rise in attacks has prompted calls for stronger security measures and international cooperation to address the root causes of extremism in the region.

articles.title: Why TSC has turned to principals to lead CBE

articles.context: The Kenyan government's shift to involve principals in leading the Competency-Based Education (CBE) system comes amid ongoing debates about educational reforms following the 2017 implementation of the new curriculum. This transition aims to address concerns raised by stakeholders, including the Kenya National Union of Teachers, about the effectiveness of CBE. The move is also significant as it aligns with President William Ruto's broader agenda to enhance educational outcomes amid rising public scrutiny.

articles.title: Road to Cricket Kenya election enters new phase as 57 delegates cleared to vote
articles.summary: Current CK chairman, Manoj Patel, has not sought re-election.
articles.context: Cricket Kenya has faced governance challenges in recent years, including a suspension from the International Cricket Council in 2020 due to internal disputes and financial mismanagement. The upcoming election, where 57 delegates will vote, marks a crucial turning point for the organization as it seeks to restore credibility and stability. With Manoj Patel stepping down, the election is pivotal for shaping the future of cricket in Kenya, especially ahead of the 2024 ICC T20 World Cup qualifiers.

articles.title: Kip Keino Classic: Women’s 100m race attracts top American sprinters

articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في سياق التوترات السياسية المستمرة في السودان بعد الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير في 2019، حيث تواجه الحكومة الانتقالية تحديات كبيرة تتعلق بالأمن والاستقرار. كما أن امتحانات الشهادة السودانية تعد حدثاً مهماً، مما دفع السلطات إلى اتخاذ تدابير غير تقليدية مثل قطع الإنترنت، في محاولة للحد من الغش وضمان نزاهة الامتحانات.

articles.context: تأتي مشاركة مي الغيطي في فيلم هوليوودي في وقت تشهد فيه السينما المصرية انتعاشًا بعد سنوات من التحديات السياسية والاقتصادية. هذا الحدث يعكس جهود الفنانين المصريين للتوسع في الأسواق العالمية، خاصة بعد توقيع اتفاقيات تعاون ثقافي بين مصر والولايات المتحدة في عام 2022، مما يسهم في تعزيز العلاقات الثقافية بين البلدين.

articles.title: Black Sea imports outpace local supply as Ethiopia battles tight grain markets
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s agricultural sector is navigating a complex paradox of record-breaking production and severe market strain. According to the latest Grain and Feed Annual report from the USDA Foreign Agricultural Service, while the nation is on track for a historic wheat harvest, structural economic shifts and high domestic costs have left the country increasingly reliant on […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's agricultural challenges are exacerbated by the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which has disrupted supply chains and agricultural production since late 2020. Additionally, the 2022 Russia-Ukraine conflict has led to increased global grain prices, pushing Ethiopia to rely on imports from the Black Sea region despite its anticipated wheat surplus. This situation highlights the tension between local production capabilities and the economic realities of a volatile global market.

articles.title: Afri Fund Capital eyes $6 billion raise for Lapsset project
articles.summary: Nairobi -based Afri Fund Capital has announced an ambitious plan to raise $6 billion (approximately 780 billion Kenyan Shillings) in commercial debt. This funding is specifically designated to complete the Lamu Port-South Sudan-Ethiopia Transport (LAPSSET) Corridor, a regional mega-project long regarded as the key to unlocking the economic potential of the Horn of Africa. The […]
articles.context: The LAPSSET Corridor, initiated in 2012, aims to enhance trade and connectivity among Kenya, South Sudan, and Ethiopia, but has faced delays due to political instability and conflict, particularly in South Sudan. The recent peace agreement in South Sudan (2020) and Kenya's strategic push for regional integration under President William Ruto's administration have renewed interest in the project, making Afri Fund Capital's $6 billion raise crucial for revitalizing economic prospects in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Sudan and Ethiopia agree to boost air connectivity
articles.summary: Sudan and Ethiopia have moved to renew civil aviation relations between the two countries. Recently, Sudan’s Ambassador to Ethiopia and Permanent Representative to the African Union, Al-Zein Ibrahim Hussein, met with the Director General of the Ethiopian Civil Aviation Authority, Yohannes Abera, in Addis Ababa for discussions. The meeting, held at the Authority’s headquarters in […]
articles.context: The renewed discussions on civil aviation come amid ongoing tensions between Sudan and Ethiopia, particularly over the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD) and border disputes. The two countries have experienced strained relations since the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in 2023, which has led to a humanitarian crisis and displacement. Strengthening air connectivity could facilitate regional cooperation and aid delivery, marking a potential thaw in relations as both nations seek stability.

articles.title: AI is fuelling the ‘Digital Colonisation’ of Africa, warns UN Scientist
articles.summary: The United Nations has launched its first global panel on artificial intelligence, as concerns grow that the technology could deepen global inequalities – particularly in Africa, where systems are largely imported after being shaped elsewhere. The panel, bringing together around 40 experts from 37 countries, was approved by the UN General Assembly in February and […]
articles.context: The launch of the UN's global panel on artificial intelligence comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing political turmoil following the Tigray conflict, which has exacerbated existing inequalities. As the country grapples with the aftermath of the 2020-2022 civil war, concerns about digital sovereignty and the reliance on foreign technology have intensified. The Ethiopian government has been seeking to develop its own digital infrastructure, highlighting the urgency of addressing the potential risks of "digital colonization."

articles.title: Social media rumors undermine law enforcement, says police chief

articles.context: The ongoing conflict in South Sudan, which erupted in 2013, has led to a fragile political environment marked by mistrust among various factions, including the government and opposition groups. The 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed to stabilize the country, but persistent violence and political instability have fueled the spread of misinformation. The rise of social media as a platform for rumors complicates law enforcement efforts, undermining public trust in the police and exacerbating tensions.

articles.title: Bar Association strategic plan designed to outlast current leadership – Nyichar
articles.summary: The South Sudan Bar Association (SSBA) has launched a new 100-day strategic plan, emphasizing that the roadmap is designed to guide the organization’s development well beyond the current leadership’s term limit. articles.context: The South Sudan Bar Association's strategic plan comes amid ongoing challenges in the country's legal framework, exacerbated by the civil war that erupted in December 2013. The peace agreement signed in September 2018 aimed to establish a transitional government, yet issues of governance and rule of law remain critical. The SSBA's initiative seeks to strengthen institutional independence and accountability as South Sudan navigates its fragile political landscape and prepares for upcoming elections.

articles.title: Senior presidential envoy Adut assesses Juba–Bahr el Ghazal road works
articles.summary: Senior Presidential Envoy Adut Salva Kiir assessed ongoing construction works on the Juba–Bahr el Ghazal highway while traveling toward Lakes State over the weekend. The road project links the capital, Juba, to Wau and other parts of the Bahr el Ghazal region. Construction on the highway resumed in January 2026 after a period of suspension. […] The post Senior presidential envoy Adut assesses Juba–Bahr el Ghazal road works first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Juba–Bahr el Ghazal highway is crucial for enhancing connectivity in South Sudan, a country that has faced prolonged civil conflict since 2013. The road's construction was initially halted due to violence and political instability but resumed in January 2026 as part of the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS), aimed at fostering peace and development. Senior Presidential Envoy Adut Salva Kiir's assessment underscores the government's commitment to infrastructure as a means of unifying the nation.

Her contributions were particularly significant during the 2018 peace agreement between Ethiopia and Eritrea, which marked a turning point in regional relations. As Ethiopia faces ongoing internal conflicts and challenges to its diplomatic standing, her legacy underscores the importance of skilled diplomacy in addressing these crises.

articles.title: Sudan calls US-Iran ceasefire ‘positive step’ toward de-escalation
articles.summary: Transitional Sovereignty Council backs diplomacy to achieve peace, stability in region
articles.context: Sudan's Transitional Sovereignty Council, formed after the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019, has been navigating a complex political landscape marked by internal strife and external pressures. The recent U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement, following heightened tensions in the region, is seen as a crucial opportunity for Sudan to stabilize its own transitional government and foster diplomatic relations, particularly as it seeks to address ongoing conflicts in Darfur and the impact of regional rivalries.

articles.title: Council of Ministers extends Tigray Interim Admin term by one year
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Council of Ministers has extended the term of Tigray Interim Administration (TIA) by one additional year. Based on the Council’s decision, PM Abiy Ahmed has approved General Tadesse Worede, to continue serving as TIA president for the next one year. This is the second time the TIA term is extended. TIA was established […]
articles.context: The Tigray Interim Administration (TIA) was established following the Tigray War, which erupted in November 2020 between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF). The conflict resulted in significant humanitarian crises and political instability in the region. The TIA's previous term was extended in 2022, and this latest extension comes amid ongoing efforts to stabilize Tigray after the peace agreement signed in November 2022, which aimed to end hostilities and restore governance.

articles.title: Election Board to deploy team to assess security situations ahead of June election
articles.summary: The National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) prepares to deploy team that will assess the security situation in polling station areas. The board convened with political parties, CSOs and stakeholders on the modality and terms of the assessment. The team will be comprised of the Board, political parties and CSOs, according to NEBE statement today. […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's political landscape has been tumultuous since the outbreak of the Tigray conflict in November 2020, which has exacerbated ethnic tensions and instability. The June 2021 elections were marred by violence and allegations of irregularities, leading to a postponement of polls in several regions. As the National Election Board of Ethiopia prepares for upcoming elections, assessing security in polling areas is crucial to ensure a more peaceful electoral process amid ongoing regional conflicts and political unrest.

articles.title: China, Russia vetoed UNSC vote over securitizing Straits of Hormuz, Bab al-Mandab
articles.summary: China and Russia vetoed UN Security Council draft resolution tabled in a bid to ensure secured use of Strait of Hormuz for commercial maritime routes. The resolution was backed by Gulf States and tabled by Bahrain. The UNSC vote took place on Tuesday April 7, 2026, the UN disclosed. Of the 15-member Security Council, 11 […]
articles.context: The Strait of Hormuz and Bab al-Mandab are critical chokepoints for global oil shipments, making their security vital for Gulf States and international trade. Tensions have escalated following the 2019 attacks on tankers in the Gulf and ongoing conflicts involving Iran and Saudi Arabia. The veto by China and Russia reflects their strategic alliances with Iran and the broader geopolitical contest in the region, particularly following the Abraham Accords and shifting U.S. foreign policy.

articles.title: New UN Rep to Ethiopia Urges Collaboration in Light of Funding Gaps
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s complex challenges require more coherent, ambitious, and system-wide collaboration to bridge humanitarian funding gaps, priority strategic engagements and coordination; says appointed UN Resident and Humanitarian Coordinator, Ozonnia Ojielo. Ojielo’s statement comes following a meeting with UNHCR Ethiopia’s leadership and staff focused on operational priorities, national alignment, and the evolving displacement landscape in Ethiopia. UNHCR […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with a humanitarian crisis exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020 and has led to widespread displacement and food insecurity. The 2022 Pretoria Agreement aimed to cease hostilities, yet challenges persist, including funding shortages for humanitarian aid. The UN's renewed focus under Ozonnia Ojielo highlights the urgent need for coordinated efforts among international agencies and the Ethiopian government to address these ongoing crises effectively.

articles.title: Djibouti’s largest port facility reports sufficient cargo handling capacity
articles.summary: Despite a growing number of cargo vessels being rerouted to Djibouti, the Doraleh Multipurpose Port (DMP)—the nation’s largest port facility—has announced that it has ample waiting and storage capacity to handle the incoming goods. The facility, which processes a broad range of import and export cargo, reports a steady increase in vessels diverting from Jebel […]
articles.context: Djibouti's strategic location at the entrance of the Red Sea has made it a critical logistics hub, especially following the 2018 Djibouti-Addis Ababa Railway agreement, which enhanced trade links with Ethiopia. The ongoing conflict in Yemen has also led to increased shipping traffic as vessels seek safer routes, further solidifying Djibouti's role in regional trade. The Doraleh Multipurpose Port's capacity to accommodate this influx is vital for maintaining stability in the Horn of Africa's economy.

articles.title: Ringleader of suspected human trafficking network arrested in Ethiopia
articles.summary: Yetbarek Dawit is alleged to have operated a multi-million-dollar operation, using torture to raise money.
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with a surge in human trafficking, exacerbated by the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020, leading to widespread displacement and vulnerability among populations. The government's ongoing efforts to combat organized crime have intensified, particularly following the 2022 peace agreement between the Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), which aimed to restore stability. Yetbarek Dawit's arrest highlights the urgent need for justice and protection for trafficking victims amid this fragile recovery.

articles.title: PM Abiy, Lt. Gen. Tadesse Convene as Tigray Admin Term Nears End
articles.summary: Lieutenant General Tadesse Werede is in Addis Ababa for a meeting with Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed as the life term of the Tigray Regional Administration nears its end. “PM Abiy Ahmed met this morning with Lt. Gen. Tadesse Worede, president of the Tigray Region Interim Administration, to receive his annual report and discuss its findings,” […]
articles.context: The meeting comes as the Tigray Regional Administration's term ends following the Tigray War, which lasted from November 2020 to November 2022 and resulted in significant humanitarian crises. The peace agreement signed in Pretoria on November 2, 2022, aimed to restore stability, but tensions remain high. Lt. Gen. Tadesse Werede's report is crucial for assessing the interim administration's progress and addressing ongoing challenges in the region.

articles.title: Birr devaluation pushes fertilizer, fuel costs higher as wheat output rises
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s sharp currency depreciation is driving up fertilizer and fuel prices, threatening to erode gains from a record wheat harvest, The report says the birr has lost about 107 per cent of its value against the US dollar since the government introduced a market-based exchange rate system in July 2024, weakening from 75 birr to […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's transition to a market-based exchange rate in July 2024 followed years of economic challenges and political unrest, including the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020. The devaluation of the birr has exacerbated inflation, impacting essential goods and agricultural inputs, despite the government's efforts to boost wheat production through the National Wheat Strategy launched in 2021. This situation highlights the fragility of economic gains amid ongoing political and social tensions.

articles.title: Guelleh Vows ‘Major Success’ as Tens of Thousands Rally for His Rule
articles.summary: President Ismail Omar Guelleh, candidate of the ruling People’s Rally for Progress (RPP) within the Union for the Presidential Majority (UMP), held a major campaign rally on the evening of April 6 at Belbala, a suburb of the capital. The open-air event drew what the president described as a significant turnout. Taking to social media […]
articles.context: President Ismail Omar Guelleh has been in power since 1999, and his rule has faced criticism for authoritarian practices and human rights abuses. The upcoming elections, scheduled for April 2021, are crucial as they follow a period of political unrest and protests against his government. Additionally, tensions with neighboring Ethiopia, particularly regarding border disputes and regional security, heighten the significance of Guelleh's campaign rally as he seeks to consolidate support amid these challenges.

articles.title: Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
articles.summary: Several footballers have absconded in the past leaving behind what rights groups call a repressive government.
articles.context: Eritrea has faced international scrutiny since its independence from Ethiopia in 1993, marked by a repressive regime under President Isaias Afwerki. The 1998-2000 Eritrean-Ethiopian War exacerbated tensions, leading to a national service policy that many view as forced conscription. The recent absconding of seven players highlights ongoing human rights concerns, as athletes often seek asylum abroad to escape the government's oppressive policies, particularly following the 2022 peace agreement between Ethiopia and Tigray forces.

articles.title: Federal Police Crack Down on Sprawling Human Trafficking Network
articles.summary: – Accused ringleader Yitbarek Dawit detained in Shire The Federal Police Commission has disclosed the arrest of several individuals linked to a human trafficking network believed to have been involved in the smuggling of thousands of people in recent years. Among the 10 individuals taken into federal custody is alleged ringleader Yitbarek Dawit (also known […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant instability since the outbreak of the Tigray conflict in November 2020, leading to increased vulnerability among displaced populations. The ongoing humanitarian crisis has created fertile ground for human trafficking networks, exploiting those fleeing violence and poverty. The recent arrest of Yitbarek Dawit and others highlights the urgent need for law enforcement to address these networks amid the government's efforts to restore order and rebuild trust in the justice system.

articles.title: Churches and politicians in South Sudan call for 'lasting peace' in Easter messages
articles.summary: There are fears South Sudan may be plunged back into a civil war less than a decade after it ended.
articles.context: South Sudan gained independence from Sudan in 2011 but descended into civil war in December 2013, primarily between factions loyal to President Salva Kiir and former Vice President Riek Machar. Despite the signing of the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan in September 2018, tensions remain high, with ongoing violence and political instability threatening the fragile peace. Recent clashes and economic challenges have heightened fears of a return to conflict as Easter approaches.

articles.title: iibGroup Receives License from NBE for a Representative Office in Addis Ababa, Strengthening Presence into Ethiopia’s Emerging Financial Sector
articles.summary: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia/Manama, Bahrain, March 27, 2026 – iibGroup has officially opened a Representative Office in Addis Ababa, marking a significant milestone in the Group’s long-term regional strategy in East Africa. The new office will enable iibGroup to deepen its engagement with Ethiopia’s financial ecosystem, support cross-border investment, and position the Group to play a […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's financial sector has been undergoing significant reforms since the 2018 appointment of Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, aimed at liberalizing the economy and attracting foreign investment. The recent signing of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement in 2021 further emphasizes Ethiopia's strategic role in regional trade. Amid ongoing conflicts in the Tigray region, iibGroup's establishment of a representative office signals confidence in Ethiopia's economic potential and stability.

articles.title: INVITATION FOR EXPRESSION OF INTEREST
articles.summary: FOR PREQUALIFICATION FOR PRE-DELIVERY INSPECTION SERVICES Due date: 29th April 2026 The United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) Ethiopia Country Office invites Request for Express of Interest (REoI) for the pre-qualification of bidders for the Pre-Delivery Inspection Service as part of UNICEF-supported programmes and operations in Ethiopia. ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS: THE PROCEDURE AND DEADLINE TO SUBMIT THE […]
articles.context: The ongoing humanitarian crisis in Ethiopia, exacerbated by the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020, has led to significant disruptions in aid delivery and essential services. UNICEF's call for pre-qualification of bidders for Pre-Delivery Inspection Services is crucial as the organization seeks to ensure quality and accountability in aid distribution amidst challenges posed by political instability and logistical hurdles. This initiative reflects UNICEF's commitment to supporting vulnerable populations in a rapidly evolving context.

articles.title: I N V I T A T I O N T O B I D
articles.summary: Sale of unserviceable Laptops, Printers, Digital Senders, Cartridges, Desktops and Monitors REF. PRO32-3-748-PMU/26-4-1 The United Nations Economic Commission for Africa (UNECA) invites interested bidders to participate in a closed bid exercise for the Sale of unserviceable Laptops, Printers, Digital Senders, Cartridges, Desktops and Monitors. The bid is to be sold “as is, where is” without […]
articles.context: The sale of unserviceable equipment by the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa (UNECA) comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The economic landscape remains fragile, with the government seeking to stabilize and rebuild. This bid reflects UNECA's efforts to streamline operations and repurpose resources in a country grappling with both humanitarian needs and economic challenges.

articles.title: Request for Quotation ( RFQ)
articles.summary: Procurement of ICT and Connectivity Equipment UNDP-ETH-00691 Negotiation: UNDP-ETH-00691 The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), in Ethiopia hereby invites you to submit your proposal to the Request for Quotation (UNDP-ETH-00691) for the Procurement of ICT and Connectivity Equipment (Laptops; Noise-Canceling Headset; Large Display Screens; Interactive Smart Display -Magic Screen; HDMI / Display Connectivity System; Network […]
articles.context: The procurement of ICT and connectivity equipment by UNDP in Ethiopia comes at a critical time as the country grapples with the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. As Ethiopia seeks to rebuild and enhance its technological infrastructure, this initiative aims to support government institutions and improve service delivery, reflecting the UN's commitment to aid in the country's recovery and development efforts.

articles.title: SOMALI REGIONAL EDUCATION BUREAU
articles.summary: Invention for local competitive bidders Somali Regional State Education Bureau invites all interested & eligible bidders for the following 1: Procurement of indoor playing and pre-primary school aged students chairs With ref: SRS/NCB/REB/0026/2018 1. Suppliers must have a valid Trading License Renewed for 2018 EFY, Tax clearance Certificate valid until the date of dead line […]
articles.context: The Somali Regional State in Ethiopia has faced ongoing challenges related to education and infrastructure, exacerbated by political tensions and ethnic conflicts, particularly following the 2018 reforms that aimed to decentralize power. The recent invitation for local bidders to supply educational materials highlights efforts to improve access to education for young children amidst these challenges, as the region seeks to stabilize and enhance its educational framework following years of instability and underinvestment.

articles.title: Somalia set for 'historic' first offshore oil drilling
articles.summary: A Turkish government-owned drilling ship is expected to arrive in Somali waters later this week.
articles.context: Somalia has faced decades of civil unrest and instability, which has hindered its ability to exploit natural resources. The 2020 agreement between the Somali government and Turkish Petroleum Corporation marked a pivotal moment in the country’s efforts to attract foreign investment in its oil sector. With the arrival of the Turkish drilling ship, Somalia aims to capitalize on its offshore oil reserves, potentially reshaping its economy and geopolitical relationships in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Somali airstrikes kill 12 al-Shabab militants near Baidoa, ministry says
articles.summary: Operation carried out with international support as campaign against insurgents intensifies
articles.context: The airstrikes near Baidoa come amid a renewed offensive against al-Shabab, which has escalated since President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's administration launched a military campaign in August 2022. This operation is part of a broader strategy supported by the African Union Transition Mission in Somalia (ATMIS) and U.S. air support, aiming to dismantle the militant group that has plagued the region for over a decade, particularly following the 2017 Mogadishu truck bombing that killed over 500 people.

articles.title: Somalia says historic oil exploration carries economic, geopolitical significance
articles.summary: Remarks come as Turkish drill ship Cagri Bey arrives in Somalia’s waters to begin 1st offshore drilling
articles.context: Somalia's oil exploration efforts have gained momentum following the 2020 signing of a production-sharing agreement with the Turkish firm Genel Energy, marking a significant step in the country's post-civil war recovery. The arrival of the Turkish drill ship Cagri Bey in October 2023 comes amid ongoing tensions with neighboring countries over maritime boundaries, particularly with Kenya, which has been a source of conflict since the International Court of Justice ruling in 2021.

articles.title: "القوميون الإنجيليون في إدارة ترامب يدمرون الجانب الأخلاقي في النظام العالمي" – مقال رأي في الغارديان
articles.summary: "بالنسبة لمعظم المسيحيين الملتزمين، فإن استغلال الدين وتشويهه وتوظيفه كسلاح لتبرير الموت والدمار، وقصف إيران "لإعادتها إلى العصور الحجرية" أمر محزن للغاية. فالمسيحيون يؤمنون بأن يسوع صُلب من أجل البشرية جمعاء، من أجل غفران الخطايا، لا من أجل الانتقام والكبرياء والهيمنة".
articles.context: تتزامن هذه المقالة مع تصاعد التوترات بين الولايات المتحدة وإيران، خاصة بعد انسحاب إدارة ترامب من الاتفاق النووي الإيراني في مايو 2018. هذا القرار أدى إلى تجديد العقوبات على إيران، مما زاد من حدة الصراع في المنطقة. في السودان، حيث يواجه المسيحيون تحديات كبيرة، يُعتبر استغلال الدين من قبل القوميين الإنجيليين في السياسة الأمريكية تهديدًا للأخلاق العالمية ويؤثر على الديناميات الإقليمية.

articles.title: Ethiopian lawyer Meron Aragaw named to Courtroom Mail 100
articles.summary: Meron Aragaw Erkihun, former Executive Director of the Ethiopian Women Lawyers Association (EWLA), has been recognized in the 2026 Courtroom Mail 100 list of top legal professionals, highlighting her contributions to women’s rights advocacy and human rights in Ethiopia. During her tenure at EWLA from 2017, Meron led efforts to promote and protect women’s economic, […]
articles.context: Meron Aragaw's recognition comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing challenges related to women's rights and human rights, particularly following the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020. The conflict exacerbated gender-based violence and highlighted the need for strong advocacy. Meron's leadership at the Ethiopian Women Lawyers Association has been crucial in addressing these issues, especially as the Ethiopian government seeks to stabilize the country and promote gender equality in the post-conflict recovery phase.

articles.title: UNICEF says Iran war means more children will suffer in Somalia
articles.summary: The sound of a crying child is a sign of hope in a crowded displacement camp in southern Somalia — the most malnourished children are too weak to even cry. For the mothers in the Ladan camp in the town of Dollow, survival is the only thing on their minds — not the Iran war […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Somalia has been exacerbated by the 2022 drought, which has left millions facing severe food insecurity. The Iran war, particularly its implications for regional stability and resource allocation, diverts international attention and aid away from Somalia, where humanitarian needs are critical. The situation is further complicated by the presence of militant groups like Al-Shabaab, which continue to undermine efforts for peace and stability in the region.

articles.title: ElectioBoard Extends Voter Registration Deadline Amid Logistical Challenges
articles.summary: The National Election Board of Ethiopia has extended the voter registration deadline to April 22, 2026, citing delays caused by online system disruptions, weak network coverage, and transportation constraints. The voter registration period for the 7th national election was initially scheduled to close on April 6, 2026, but the Board said the extension was necessary […]
articles.context: The extension of the voter registration deadline comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing political tensions following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which has strained the electoral process. The National Election Board's decision reflects concerns over ensuring a fair and inclusive election, especially after the postponement of the 2021 elections due to security issues. The upcoming 7th national election is crucial for stabilizing the country, as it aims to restore public trust in the democratic process.

articles.title: At least 9 al-Shabaab terrorists killed during intelligence operation in Somalia
articles.summary: During operation in Lower Shabelle region, 6 heavy engines used by al-Qaeda-affiliated terrorist group to manufacture explosives also destroyed, says Intelligence and Security Agency
articles.context: The operation in Lower Shabelle comes amid escalating violence in Somalia, where al-Shabaab has intensified attacks against government forces and civilians. The Somali government, supported by African Union forces, has been conducting a renewed offensive against the group since early 2022, following the election of President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud. This operation highlights the ongoing struggle to dismantle al-Shabaab's operational capabilities and restore stability in a region plagued by decades of conflict.

articles.title: Asymmetric Conflict in Somalia, Sudan: How it Affects Ethiopia
articles.summary: This week, Somalia’s national army announced it has taken control of the city of Baidowa in the country’s South West State. Located just 150 miles north-west of the capital Mogadishu, Baidowa is a strategic city that has served as a base for international peacekeepers, including Ethiopian troops, and humanitarian agencies in an area affected by […]
articles.context: Baidowa's capture is significant as it underscores the ongoing struggle between Somalia's federal government and the militant group Al-Shabaab, which has long threatened stability in the region. The Ethiopian National Defense Force (ENDF) has historically been involved in peacekeeping efforts in Somalia, particularly following the 2012 agreement between the African Union and Somali authorities to combat terrorism. The recent developments may impact Ethiopia's security posture and its role in regional stability amid the ongoing conflict in Sudan.

articles.title: Justice Ministry Seeks ETB 9.5 bln to Back Five-Year Reforms
articles.summary: Conflict, economic crime, corruption cited as major bottlenecks Officials at the Ministry of Justice say they require close to 10 billion Birr to implement an overhaul of the legal system before the decade is out. A new five-year plan issued by the Ministry seeks 9.5 billion Birr, one billion of which officials hope to source […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with internal conflict since the Tigray War erupted in November 2020, leading to widespread human rights abuses and a breakdown of legal institutions. The ongoing tensions between the federal government and regional forces, particularly in Tigray and Oromia, have exacerbated issues of corruption and economic crime. The proposed 9.5 billion Birr reform plan is critical for restoring public trust and stabilizing the legal framework amid these challenges.

articles.title: Double Taxation, Contraband Eat into Khat, Oilseed Export Earnings
articles.summary: The Ministry of Trade and Regional Integration reports that contraband trade and taxes levied by regional administrations have cut into earnings from the export of khat and oilseeds. Reporting to Parliament this week, officials told lawmakers that despite a record USD 6.7 billion in export revenues over the first eight months of the financial year, […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's khat and oilseed exports have historically been vital to its economy, especially amid ongoing conflicts in the Tigray region that have strained resources and trade routes. The federal government's recent push for regional administrations to streamline taxation comes after the 2020 Tigray conflict, which disrupted agricultural production and trade. As the country seeks to stabilize its economy post-conflict, the impact of double taxation and contraband on export earnings has become a pressing concern for lawmakers.

articles.title: Ethiopia Secures Hague Victory in Lease Arbitration Case
articles.summary: The Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia has secured a decisive victory in a 334 million euro investment arbitration brought by African Asset Finance Company Holdings B.V., a Netherlands-incorporated entity wholly owned by a Delaware-based parent company. This week, a tribunal constituted under the UN Commission on International Trade Law (UNCITRAL) dismissed all claims tied to […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's victory in this arbitration case comes amid ongoing tensions related to foreign investments in the country, particularly in the context of the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam dispute with downstream countries like Egypt and Sudan. The ruling, delivered on October 10, 2023, is significant as it reinforces Ethiopia's legal position against foreign claims, reflecting its broader strategy to assert sovereignty over its resources while navigating complex international relations and economic challenges.

articles.title: Fuel Crisis Exposes Ethiopia’s Energy Fragility
articles.summary: Ethiopia is grappling with a fuel shortage that has rippled through every corner of the economy, a crisis triggered by the U.S.-Israel war with Iran and the disruption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The blockade has choked off a significant portion of global oil flows, and for Ethiopia—entirely dependent on imports—the consequences have […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's reliance on imported fuel has intensified amid the ongoing U.S.-Israel tensions with Iran, particularly following the outbreak of conflict in October 2023. The disruption of oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz has exacerbated the situation, as Ethiopia's energy sector struggles to cope with rising demand and limited supply. This crisis comes on the heels of the Ethiopian government's efforts to stabilize its economy post-conflict, particularly after the Tigray War, which ended in November 2022.

articles.title: Ethiopia Faces Multi-Crisis Stress Test as Overlapping Shocks Threaten Stability
articles.summary: Policy analysis urges shift from reactive crisis response to long-term resilience planning A policy analysis published this week by the Institute of Foreign Affairs (IFA) warns that Ethiopia is undergoing a “multi-disruption stress test,” as overlapping economic, environmental and security challenges place mounting pressure on the country’s governance systems. The study, authored by Amran Gamachu […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is grappling with the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022, yet tensions remain high in regions like Oromia and Amhara. The country faces severe economic challenges, exacerbated by the global impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic and the ongoing effects of climate change, including droughts and locust invasions. These factors, combined with political instability, threaten the fragile governance structures established post-conflict.

articles.title: Wingu Africa Launches Wingu Cloud Exchange in Ethiopia
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Wingu Africa, a major carrier-neutral, Tier III-standard data centre operator, announced the launch of the Wingu Cloud Exchange (WCX), a new private cloud platform tailored
articles.context: The launch of Wingu Cloud Exchange in Ethiopia comes amid the country's ongoing efforts to enhance its digital infrastructure following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which disrupted telecommunications and internet access. As Ethiopia seeks to attract foreign investment and improve its technological capabilities, this initiative aligns with the government's Digital Ethiopia 2025 strategy, aimed at fostering a competitive digital economy and reducing reliance on foreign cloud services.

articles.title: IMF Chief Appoints Mauritania’s Zeine Zeidane as African Department Director
articles.summary: Mauritania’s Zeine Zeidane has been appointed as Director of the African Department (AFR) at the International Monetary Fund (IMF). Zeidane will succeed Abebe Aemro Selassie, who will retire
articles.context: Zeine Zeidane's appointment comes at a crucial time for African economies, particularly as the region grapples with the aftermath of the COVID-19 pandemic and rising inflation. Ethiopia, facing internal conflict and economic challenges, is part of a broader regional dynamic where effective leadership at the IMF is vital for securing financial support and fostering stability. The transition follows the 2021 African Union-led ceasefire agreement in Ethiopia, highlighting the need for strategic economic guidance.

articles.title: MIDROC, TFG Hospitality Sign Master Agreement for 10 Properties in Ethiopia
articles.summary: MIDROC Investment Group and The First Group Hospitality, a Dubai-headquartered hospitality management firm, signed a master agreement to develop and operate 10 properties across Ethiopia. The portfolio includes
articles.context: The signing of the master agreement between MIDROC Investment Group and The First Group Hospitality comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from a brutal civil conflict that erupted in November 2020, primarily involving the Tigray region. This agreement, finalized in October 2023, signals a renewed focus on economic development and foreign investment in Ethiopia, particularly as the government seeks to stabilize the country and attract international partners following the Tigray Peace Agreement in November 2022.

articles.title: NBE, China’ Central Banks Discuss Advancing Monetary Cooperation
articles.summary: National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE) says its Governor Eyob Tekalign met with People’s Bank of China (PBOC) governor Pan Gongsheng on Thursday to advance a monetary cooperation aimed
articles.context: The meeting between NBE Governor Eyob Tekalign and PBOC Governor Pan Gongsheng comes at a critical time as Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy amid ongoing challenges from the Tigray conflict and inflation. China's increasing investment in Ethiopia, highlighted by the Belt and Road Initiative, underscores the importance of strengthening financial ties, particularly as Ethiopia navigates its post-conflict recovery and aims to attract foreign investment to boost economic growth.

articles.title: Sixteen Kenyans missing in Russia after army recruitment
articles.summary: More than 250 Kenyans have gone to fight for Russia in Ukraine, mostly willingly, Kenya's foreign minister says.
articles.context: The recruitment of Kenyans to fight for Russia in Ukraine comes amid heightened tensions following Russia's invasion of Ukraine in February 2022. This situation reflects a broader trend of foreign fighters joining the conflict, with Kenya's foreign minister, Alfred Mutua, acknowledging the voluntary nature of this enlistment. The Kenyan government is now grappling with the implications of its citizens engaging in a foreign war, raising concerns about national security and diplomatic relations with Western nations.

articles.title: ‘teleSign’: Ethio telecom Rolls out Digital Signature Platform
articles.summary: Ethio Telecom has launched a national digital signature platform, teleSign, in a move that amed at enabling secure online identity verification and expanding access to government services without
articles.context: The launch of teleSign comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing efforts to modernize its digital infrastructure following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which highlighted vulnerabilities in identity verification and access to services. As the government seeks to enhance transparency and rebuild trust, the digital signature platform aligns with the broader national agenda initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's administration to promote digital governance and improve public service delivery, particularly in a post-conflict context.

articles.title: "كنت أسمع أصواتهم وهم يحترقون".. شهادات من سكان مدينة الدلنج السودانية
articles.summary: تتعرض الدلنج لقصف مدفعي متكرر، وسط اتهامات لقوات الدعم السريع المتحالفة مع الحركة الشعبية لتحرير السودان – شمال باستهداف الأحياء السكنية
articles.context: تعيش مدينة الدلنج في ولاية جنوب كردفان السودانية حالة من التوتر المتزايد منذ اندلاع النزاع بين قوات الدعم السريع والحركة الشعبية لتحرير السودان – شمال في عام 2011. تصاعدت حدة القصف المدفعي في الأسابيع الأخيرة، مما أدى إلى نزوح السكان وارتفاع عدد الضحايا، في ظل غياب أي اتفاقية سلام فعالة منذ اتفاقية أديس أبابا في 2011. هذه الأحداث تأتي في وقت حساس حيث تتزايد الضغوط الدولية لحل النزاع.

articles.title: Somalia registers ship under national flag after over 3 decades
articles.summary: Registration represents 'the revival of Somalia’s maritime authority and the renewed credibility of the Somali flag,' says ports and marine transport minister
articles.context: Somalia's maritime sector has been largely dormant since the collapse of the central government in 1991, leading to rampant piracy and illegal fishing off its coast. The recent registration of a ship under the Somali flag marks a pivotal moment in the country's efforts to rebuild its maritime authority, coinciding with ongoing peace initiatives and the establishment of a federal government that seeks to enhance national sovereignty and economic development.

articles.title: Ethiopian Airlines Resumes Flights to Middle East Destinations
articles.summary: Ethiopian Airlines, Africa’s largest flight carrier, has resumed flights to some destinations in the Middle East after weeks of disruption caused by the conflict in the region. Mesfin
articles.context: The resumption of Ethiopian Airlines flights to the Middle East comes after a period of heightened tensions following the outbreak of conflict in Ethiopia's Tigray region in November 2020, which has since expanded to involve various ethnic groups and regional actors. The African Union's peace talks, initiated in October 2022, aimed to stabilize the situation, but ongoing violence and political instability have continued to disrupt air travel and economic ties with Middle Eastern countries.

articles.title: DR Congo Book First World Cup Spot in 52-year with Victory over Jamaica
articles.summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo have qualified for the 2026 FIFA World Cup after beating Jamaica 1-0 in their intercontinental play-off final in Mexico. The historic victory on
articles.context: The Democratic Republic of Congo's qualification for the 2026 FIFA World Cup comes amid ongoing political instability and conflict in the eastern regions, where armed groups have long contested control. This achievement is particularly significant as it represents a unifying moment for a nation grappling with the aftermath of the 1998-2003 Congo Wars and ongoing tensions, including the recent peace agreements aimed at stabilizing the region, such as the Nairobi Process initiated in 2022.

articles.title: Ethiopia Advances to AfCON 2027 Qualifying Round
articles.summary: Ethiopia sealed progress to the next stage of the Africa Cup of Nations PAMOJA 2027 qualifiers, beating Sao Tome and Principe 4-0 on aggregate in their preliminary round
articles.context: Ethiopia's advancement in the AfCON 2027 qualifiers comes amid ongoing political tensions and ethnic conflicts within the country, particularly following the Tigray War that officially ended in November 2022. The national team's success serves as a unifying force, promoting national pride and stability in a time of uncertainty. Additionally, Ethiopia's participation in the tournament reflects its efforts to strengthen regional ties within the East African Community, fostering cooperation through sports.

articles.title: Eritrea advance after 19-year absence from Afcon qualifying
articles.summary: Eritrea are one of six nations to reach the group stage of qualification for Afcon 2027, having played their first games in the competition since 2007.
articles.context: Eritrea's return to the Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) qualifying stage comes after a prolonged absence due to internal political strife and regional tensions, particularly with Ethiopia. The 1998-2000 Eritrean-Ethiopian War and subsequent border conflicts have hampered Eritrea's sports development. The recent peace agreement between Eritrea and Ethiopia in 2018 has fostered a more stable environment, allowing Eritrea to re-engage in international competitions, marking a significant moment for national pride and unity.

articles.title: More than 70 miners killed in South Sudan as government and opposition trade blame
articles.summary: Both government and opposition groups have been accused of being responsible for the deadly attack.
articles.context: The deadly attack on miners in South Sudan occurs against the backdrop of ongoing tensions between the government, led by President Salva Kiir, and opposition forces loyal to Riek Machar, stemming from a civil war that erupted in 2013. Despite the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending hostilities, sporadic violence continues, particularly in resource-rich areas, exacerbating the humanitarian crisis and complicating the fragile political landscape.

articles.title: Commercial flights resume to Somalia's South West state after nearly 2-week halt
articles.summary: South West state had suspended flights over dispute with Mogadishu on state president Laftagareen's mandate
articles.context: The suspension of flights to Somalia's South West state stemmed from a political dispute between the regional government, led by President Abdiaziz Laftagareen, and the federal government in Mogadishu over the legitimacy of Laftagareen's mandate, which has been contested since his election in 2020. This conflict highlights ongoing tensions between federal and regional authorities, exacerbated by a lack of consensus on power-sharing agreements established during the 2012 Provisional Constitution.

articles.title: Sexual violence part of 'everyday life' in parts of Sudan, charity says
articles.summary: Victims describe how they have been targeted while carrying out everyday activities.
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, exacerbated by the 2021 military coup led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, has created a power vacuum and intensified violence, particularly in regions like Darfur. The collapse of civil order and the resurgence of armed groups have led to widespread human rights abuses, including sexual violence, as reported by organizations like Amnesty International. This situation is further complicated by the lack of accountability and the international community's limited response.

articles.title: AU Extends Condolence after Passing of former Algerian President Zeroual
articles.summary: African Union Commission Chairperson Mahmoud Ali Youssouf has extended his heartfelt condolences over the passing former Algerian President Liamine Zeroual on Sunday. Former President Zéroual died at the
articles.context: Liamine Zeroual served as Algeria's president from 1994 to 1999 during a tumultuous period marked by the Algerian Civil War, which claimed over 200,000 lives. His leadership was pivotal in initiating the "Civil Concord" policy in 1999, aimed at reconciling the nation post-conflict. His passing comes at a time when Algeria is navigating regional tensions, particularly concerning its relationships with neighboring countries and the ongoing instability in the Sahel region.

articles.title: Korir banned for five years over doping admission
articles.summary: Former New York marathon winner Albert Korir of Kenya is banned for five years after admitting to doping.
articles.context: Albert Korir's five-year ban comes amid heightened scrutiny of doping in athletics, particularly in Kenya, which has faced numerous scandals involving its elite runners. The country has been under investigation by the Athletics Integrity Unit since 2016, with over 50 athletes banned for doping violations. Korir's admission reflects ongoing challenges in maintaining integrity in a sport where Kenya has historically excelled, particularly following the Tokyo 2020 Olympics, where doping allegations overshadowed the nation's achievements.

articles.title: African Union urges peaceful dialogue in Somalia amid federal government, South West state tensions
articles.summary: Bloc ‘stands ready to support dialogue and reconciliation,’ says statement
articles.context: Tensions between Somalia's federal government and the South West state have escalated following the controversial election of the South West president in 2021, which was marred by allegations of fraud and political manipulation. The ongoing rift has hindered efforts to stabilize the region and address security threats from groups like Al-Shabaab. The African Union's call for dialogue comes as the country prepares for national elections, emphasizing the need for reconciliation to ensure a peaceful electoral process.

articles.title: One ant for $220: The new frontier of wildlife trafficking
articles.summary: The craze for collecting ants takes Kenya by surprise as smugglers zone in to make a profit.
articles.context: In recent years, Kenya has seen a surge in wildlife trafficking, exacerbated by the rise of social media platforms that facilitate the illegal trade of exotic species, including ants. The 2019 ban on the export of certain wildlife species has intensified the demand for rare specimens, leading to a black market where prices soar. This trend reflects broader issues of biodiversity loss and the challenges faced by Kenya's Wildlife Service in enforcing conservation laws amidst limited resources.

articles.title: Abel Scores Twice as Ethiopia Beat Sao Tome e Principe in AfCON 2027 Qualifying Opener
articles.summary: Ethiopia took major step forwards to the next stage of CAF Africa Cup of Nations PAMOJA 2027 qualifiers with a 3-0 win over Sao Tome e Principe in
articles.context: Ethiopia's victory over Sao Tome e Principe comes amidst a challenging backdrop of internal conflict and political instability, particularly following the Tigray War, which officially ended in November 2022. The Ethiopian national team’s success in the AFCON 2027 qualifiers is seen as a unifying moment for the country, fostering national pride and solidarity as it seeks to recover from recent turmoil and enhance its international standing in sports.

articles.title: NBE Launches Hunts for Consulting Firm to Design New Headquarters
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Ethiopia’s Central Bank has put a call out to consulting firms to undertake architectural and engineering design for a New Headquarter construction project. The National
articles.context: The National Bank of Ethiopia's initiative to construct a new headquarters comes amid ongoing economic challenges and a push for modernization in the wake of the 2020 Tigray conflict, which strained the country's financial systems. The government aims to bolster investor confidence and stabilize the economy, particularly as it seeks to implement the Homegrown Economic Reform agenda initiated in 2020. This project reflects Ethiopia's broader efforts to enhance institutional infrastructure and attract foreign investment.

articles.title: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
articles.summary: Women in Kenya and Ghana tell the BBC about being approached by a Russian man who later posts videos of them without their consent.
articles.context: In recent years, Kenya and Ghana have faced increasing challenges related to online privacy and digital exploitation, exacerbated by the rise of social media. The 2020 Cybersecurity Act in Kenya aimed to address online harassment, yet incidents persist, highlighting enforcement gaps. Meanwhile, Ghana's 2018 Data Protection Act has struggled to keep pace with rapid technological changes, leaving many vulnerable to exploitation by foreign actors, such as the Russian man implicated in these recent abuses.

articles.title: Somali forces kill over 42 al-Shabaab terrorists, including 2 ringleaders, in military operations
articles.summary: Abdihamid Ali Abdullahi Macalin, Suleyman Omar Fidow, among al-Qaeda-linked terror group's members, killed in military operations in Middle Juba and Lower Shabelle regions, says official statement
articles.context: The recent military operations in Somalia come amid heightened efforts by the Somali National Army to combat al-Shabaab, particularly following the 2020 agreement between the Somali government and regional states to intensify counter-terrorism efforts. The killing of key ringleaders Abdihamid Ali Abdullahi Macalin and Suleyman Omar Fidow is significant as it disrupts al-Shabaab's operational capabilities and reflects ongoing U.S. support for Somali forces in their fight against the group, which has escalated since the withdrawal of AMISOM troops in 2021.

articles.title: Libya says drifting Russian tanker moved to international waters off coast
articles.summary: National Oil Corporation tasked with safely unloading vessel after operation
articles.context: The drifting Russian tanker off Libya's coast comes amid heightened tensions in the Eastern Mediterranean, where energy exploration has intensified following the 2019 Libyan ceasefire. The National Oil Corporation's involvement reflects ongoing challenges in securing oil resources amidst the power struggle between the Government of National Unity and rival factions. Additionally, the situation is complicated by Russia's increasing influence in the region, particularly through military and economic ties established since the Libyan conflict escalated in 2011.

articles.title: UNGA Adopts Resolution Declaring Slave Trade as “Gravest Crime against Humanity”
articles.summary: UNITED NATIONS – The UN General Assembly (UNGA) on Wednesday adopted a draft resolution of “Declaration of the Trafficking of Enslaved Africans and Racialized Chattel Enslavement of Africans
articles.context: The adoption of the UNGA resolution comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing efforts to address historical injustices related to slavery and human trafficking, particularly in the context of the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020. The resolution aligns with Ethiopia's commitment to the African Union's Agenda 2063, which emphasizes the importance of human rights and dignity. Additionally, it reflects a growing international focus on reparative justice for the descendants of enslaved Africans, particularly in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Tanzania’s Former President Named AU Envoy for the Horn of Africa, Red Sea
articles.summary: The Chairperson of the African Union Commission Chair Mahmoud Ali Youssouf, has appointed Tanzania’s former president Jakaya Mrisho Kikwete as High Representative for the Horn of Africa and
articles.context: Jakaya Kikwete's appointment comes at a critical time as the Horn of Africa grapples with ongoing conflicts, including the Tigray War in Ethiopia, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. His role is significant as regional tensions persist, particularly involving Ethiopia, Eritrea, and Somalia, and as the African Union seeks to bolster diplomatic efforts to stabilize the region amid humanitarian crises and political unrest.

articles.title: Ethiopian Signs Lease Deal with AerCap for two Boeing 777-300ERSFs
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Ethiopian Airlines has signed lease agreements with AerCap Holdings N.V. for two Boeing 777-300ERSF converted aircrafts. The aircraft, also known as “The Big Twin,” will
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines, Africa's largest airline, is expanding its fleet amidst ongoing regional tensions, particularly with the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) conflict that has impacted the economy since late 2020. The lease of the Boeing 777-300ERSFs comes as the airline seeks to enhance its cargo capabilities to support trade routes, especially in light of the recent peace agreement signed in November 2022, which aims to stabilize the region and boost economic recovery.

articles.title: Medical group accuses RSF of killing 16 civilians in Sudan’s Darfur
articles.summary: Group says victims were executed and buried in mass graves, calls for urgent international action
articles.context: The reported killings in Darfur come amid escalating violence following the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). This conflict has reignited long-standing ethnic tensions and instability in the region, which has faced atrocities since the Darfur genocide in the early 2000s. The international community, including the African Union and UN, has been urged to intervene as civilian casualties continue to rise.

articles.title: UN chief 'strongly' condemns deadly drone strike on hospital in Sudan, calls for ceasefire
articles.summary: Guterres calls on parties to 'immediately' de-escalate fighting and agree on cessation of hostilities, his spokesman says
articles.context: The recent drone strike on a hospital in Sudan occurs amid escalating violence between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, which began in April 2023. This conflict has resulted in widespread humanitarian crises, with over 5 million people displaced. The UN-brokered ceasefire agreements, including the Jeddah process initiated in May 2023, have repeatedly failed, highlighting the urgent need for international intervention and a sustainable resolution to the ongoing violence.

articles.title: ‘Dine for Generations’: PM Abiy Inaugurates Niin Lee Palm Lodge in Afar Region
articles.summary: Niin Lee Palm Lodge, a new tourist destination developed through the Dine for Generations initiative, has been officially opened in the Afar region. The inauguration on Sunday comes
articles.context: The inauguration of Niin Lee Palm Lodge is significant amid ongoing efforts to revitalize Ethiopia's tourism sector following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which severely impacted the economy and regional stability. The Dine for Generations initiative aims to promote sustainable development and foster peace in the Afar region, which has historically faced challenges from both internal conflicts and climate change. This development aligns with the Ethiopian government's broader strategy to enhance tourism as a means of economic recovery.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s Inflation Rate Stands at 9.7% in February
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – The Consumer Price Index (CPI) for February rose at 9.7% rate from the same time a year ago, according to the Ethiopian Statistics Services (ESS)
articles.context: Ethiopia's inflation rate has been a growing concern amid ongoing economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The conflict disrupted supply chains and agricultural production, contributing to rising prices. Additionally, the government’s efforts to stabilize the economy through reforms and international support are critical as the nation seeks recovery from the economic fallout of the civil war and the COVID-19 pandemic.

articles.title: Why acknowledging slavery is not enough without reparatory justice
articles.summary: Ghana urges the UN to recognize the transatlantic slave trade as a crime against humanity and pursue reparatory justice.

articles.title: عيد آخر بلا ديار.. السودانيون بين لوعة الفقد ومتاهات النزوح واللجوء
articles.summary: بورتسودان 20 مارس 2026 – عيدٌ آخر والسودانيون يفقدون الأحبة، والأهل تائهون في مواقع النزوح واللجوء، بيننا تقترب الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع من عامها الرابع، تلك الذكرى التي ستحل بين عيدين. ومع استمرار القتال وتحليق المسيرات في أنحاء البلاد، وما تخلفه من أوضاع كارثية، يحل عيد الفطر على السودانيين بين نازح ولاجئ وعائدين بلا شيء. وأشار العديد من السودانيين على حساباتهم في وسائل التواصل الاجتماعي إلى اتساع رقعة المأساة التي رسمتها حرب 15 أبريل 2023 مع حلول عيد الفطر اليوم الجمعة. وقالت الباحثة الاجتماعية د.

articles.title: نيكولاس “فينك” هايسوم.. رحيل إنسان حقيقي ومناضل أفريقي وحدوي
articles.summary: ياسر عرمان لطالما أبهرني الجنوب أفريقيون البيض الذين امتلكوا الرؤية والشجاعة للوقوف ضد نظام الفصل العنصري والنضال من أجل مجتمع لا عنصري. وعلى وجه الخصوص، المحامون العظماء والقادة التقدميون من البيض، مثل برام فيشر، وجورج بيزوس، ونيكولاس “فينك” هايسوم. كان فينك إنسانًا حقيقيًا ووحدويًا أفريقيًا ملتزمًا وقف بثبات ضد نظام الفصل العنصري. ومن بين صفاته العديدة دعك عن المناصب التي شغلها، بما فيها كونه مستشارًا للرئيس نيلسون مانديلا، ومساهمته في العديد من عمليات السلام مثل عملية السلام في نيفاشا وعملية تاميل تايغر في سيرلانكا، فقد ركّز على قضايا الإصلاح والحكم الديمقراطي والمصالحة وقضايا العدالة.

articles.title: Standard Group, Mediamax journalists attacked amid fake ambulance scam coverage
articles.summary: Standard Group and Mediamax journalists assaulted while covering a UDA meeting in Cherangany, Trans Nzoia; attack followed story on a fake ambulance deal at Harambee House.

articles.title: الجيش السوداني والحركة الإسلامية بين القطيعة والمناورة
articles.summary: محمد الامين عبد النبي تشهد الساحة السياسية والعسكرية في السودان مرحلة شديدة التعقيد، تتداخل فيها الحسابات العسكرية مع الصراعات السياسية، في ظل تصاعد التوتر بين قيادة الجيش السوداني والحركة الإسلامية. هذا التوتر الذي ظل لسنوات طويلة مستتراً خلف تحالفات الضرورة وتقاطعات المصالح، بدأ في الآونة الأخيرة يخرج إلى العلن تدريجياً، خاصة بعد تصنيف الحركة الإسلامية كمنظمة إرهابية، وهي خطوة أعادت ترتيب خطوط التماس بين الطرفين وأطلقت ديناميات صراع جديدة داخل معسكري الجيش والإسلاميين على حد سواء. لم يكن التحالف بين المؤسسة العسكرية والحركة الإسلامية أمراً طارئاً في تاريخ السودان الحديث. فمنذ انقلاب الانقاذ الذي أوصل نظام الإسلاميين إلى السلطة، تشكلت...

articles.title: السودان يستعد لاحتمالات شح وغلاء الأدوية مع استمرار الحرب على إيران
articles.summary: الخرطوم 20 مارس 2026 – تثير الحرب على إيران الدائرة منذ نحو ثلاثة أسابيع مخاوف من انعكاسات خطيرة على إمدادات الأدوية وارتفاع تكاليف التصنيع والاستيراد، وسط توقعات بتفاقم آثارها على السودان ما اضطر السلطات للاستعداد لأسواء الاحتمالات. ويعاني السودان أصلا من تدمير قطاع التصنيع الدوائي وشح النقد الأجنبي. وتضرر نحو 85% من القطاع الصناعي في السودان جراء الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع منذ 2023، بما في ذلك مصانع الأدوية بولاية الخرطوم، التي كانت تنتج نحو 80 صنفا دوائيا، حيث تعرض بعضها للتدمير، بينما توقف إنتاج البعض الآخر بسبب انعدام الأمن، وفقًا لإحصاءات رسمية. وعقد وزير الصحة السوداني هيثم محمد إبراهيم، الأسبوع الماضي، الاجتماع...

articles.title: قائد الدعم السريع يجدد القبول بهدنة إنسانية «جادة»
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 20 مارس 2026 – جدد قائد قوات الدعم السريع محمد حمدان دقلو حميدتي، الجمعة، موافقته الصريحة على هدنة جادة وذات مصداقية للأغراض الإنسانية الخالصة. وقال حميدتي في كلمة له بمناسبة عيد الفطر إن الهدنة الإنسانية يجب أن تكفل وصول المساعدات إلى المدنيين بلا عوائق، وفتح الممرات الآمنة وتخفيف معاناة المتضررين وحماية النازحين واللاجئين. وأضاف قائد الدعم السريع أن تنفيذ الهدنة يجب ان يضمن عدم استغلالها لمآرب عسكرية أو سياسية ضيقة – حسب تعبيره -. وكان قائد الجيش السوداني عبد الفتاح البرهان قد جدد أمس رفضه لأي هدنة أو وقف إطلاق نار قبل أن تتجمع قوات الدعم السريع في مناطق بعينها وتسلم أسلحتها.

articles.title: Troika, aid agencies raise alarm over escalating violence in South Sudan
articles.summary: Aid agencies and Western diplomats warn of a worsening humanitarian crisis in South Sudan’s Jonglei region as conflict escalates.

articles.title: Rwanda church strips 35 pastors over genocide convictions
articles.summary: Rwanda’s Pentecostal church has removed 35 pastors and evangelists convicted of genocide, signalling tighter accountability as the country approaches its annual commemoration.

articles.title: Kigali beats Nairobi in battle of financial hubs
articles.summary: Kigali is outpacing Nairobi as East Africa’s financial hub, leveraging aggressive tax incentives and regulatory efficiency to attract global capital, while Kenya scrambles to close the gap.

articles.title: مسيرات الدعم السريع تهاجم الدبة بشمال السودان فجر عيد الفطر
articles.summary: الدبة، 20 مارس 2026 – هاجمت مسيرات استراتيجية تابعة لقوات الدعم السريع، الجمعة، مدينة الدبة شمالي السودان مما أدى لانقطاع التيار الكهربائي إثر غارات استهدفت محيط محطة كهرباء المدينة. وقال المدير التنفيذي لمحلية الدبة بالولاية الشمالية ورئيس اللجنة الأمنية بالمحلية محمد صابر إن قوات الدعم السريع استهدفت صباح الجمعة وفي أول أيام عيد الفطر مدينة الدبة بعدد من المسيرات الاستراتيجية. وأكد المدير التنفيذي للمحلية في تصريح صحفي أن المسيرات استهدفت عددا من الأعيان المدنية وكلية الهندسة مما أدى إلى إصابة ثلاثة أشخاص بجروح. وأوضح أن قوات الدعم السريع كعادتها تستهدف الأعيان المدنية لزرع الهلع والخوف في قلوب المواطنين العزل بالدبة أول أيام عيد الفطر.

articles.title: Mombasa, Lamu dock more ships as Dar lags
articles.summary: Gulf conflict reroutes thousands of vehicles to Mombasa and Lamu, straining ports and threatening East African exports.

articles.title: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
articles.summary: Some 26 million people are "facing extreme hunger" in Kenya, Ethiopia and Somalia, warns humanitarian organisation Oxfam.
articles.context: The ongoing drought in the Horn of Africa, exacerbated by climate change and the 2020 locust invasion, has severely impacted food security. The conflict in Ethiopia's Tigray region, which began in November 2020, has further strained humanitarian efforts, complicating aid distribution. As of October 2023, the situation remains dire, with Oxfam reporting that 26 million people are facing extreme hunger across Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia, highlighting the urgent need for intervention.

articles.title: السلطات التشادية تبدأ حملة قمع أمنية واسعة في الحدود مع السودان

articles.summary: بارا 18 مارس 2026 – قالت شبكة أطباء السودان، الأربعاء، إن قوات الدعم السريع اغتالت 12 مدنيا بينهم نساء وأصابت 3 آخرين بذريعة الانتماء للجيش، في منطقة (شريم ميما) شمالي مدينة بارا بولاية شمال كردفان. وتبادل كل من الجيش السوداني والدعم السريع السيطرة على بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان عدة مرات، قبل أن تسيطر عليها قوات الدعم السريع هذا الأسبوع. وأفادت الشبكة في بيان، إن “الدعم السريع ارتكبت مجزرة جديدة في منطقة شريم ميما شمالي مدينة بارا، حيث صفّت 12 شخصا بينهم 6 نساء، وأصابت 3 آخرين بجروح متفاوتة، بتهمة انتمائهم إلى الجيش السوداني”. وأشارت الشبكة إلى أن استهداف المدنيين العزّل يمثل انتهاكا صارخا للقوانين والأعراف الإنسانية والدولية.

articles.title: «بنك السودان»: عجز الميزان التجاري في 2025 بلغ 3.8 مليار دولار
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 16 مارس 2026 – كشف بنك السودان المركزي، الاثنين، عن اتساع العجز في الميزان التجاري للسودان خلال العام الماضي إلى 3.8 مليار دولار. وظل السودان يسجل عجزًا في الميزان التجاري كل عام، يتراوح بين 3 إلى 6 مليارات دولار، قبل أن ينخفض في 2024 إلى 1.7 مليار دولار. وقال البنك المركزي، في تقرير الموجز الإحصائي للتجارة الخارجية لعام 2025، إن “أجمالي الصادرات السودانية بلغ نحو 2.64 مليار دولار، مقابل واردات بلغت 6.49 مليار دولار، مما أدى إلى تسجيل عجز تجاري قدره نحو 3.86 مليار دولار”. وأوضح أن قيمة الصادرات البالغة 2.639 مليار دولار جاءت نتيجة هيمنة قطاع المعادن والمنتجات الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية.
